solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int maxFreq(String s, int maxLetters, int minSize, int maxSize) {
        Map<String, Integer> substringCount = new HashMap<>();
        Map<Character, Integer> uniqueCharCount = new HashMap<>();

        int left = 0;
        int result = 0;

        for (int right = 0; right < s.length(); right++) {
            char rightChar = s.charAt(right);
            uniqueCharCount.put(rightChar, uniqueCharCount.getOrDefault(rightChar, 0) + 1);

            // Check if substring length is at least minSize
            if (right - left + 1 < minSize) {
                continue;
            }

            // Shrink window from the left if needed
            while (right - left + 1 > maxSize || uniqueCharCount.size() > maxLetters) {
                char leftChar = s.charAt(left);
                uniqueCharCount.put(leftChar, uniqueCharCount.get(leftChar) - 1);
                if (uniqueCharCount.get(leftChar) == 0) {
                    uniqueCharCount.remove(leftChar);
                }
                left++;
            }

            // Only consider substrings of minSize since longer substrings won't give more occurrences
            if (right - left + 1 == minSize && uniqueCharCount.size() <= maxLetters) {
                String currentSubstring = s.substring(left, right + 1);
                substringCount.put(currentSubstring, substringCount.getOrDefault(currentSubstring, 0) + 1);
                result = Math.max(result, substringCount.get(currentSubstring));
            }
        }

        return result;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n * minSize)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a sliding window approach to efficiently count substrings of size `minSize` (since longer substrings won't occur more frequently). It maintains a map to count occurrences of valid substrings and another to track unique characters in the current window. The key strength is focusing on `minSize` substrings, which reduces unnecessary computation.
